notes: Early WOTC set (released 2000-02-24, pre-2002) so NO reverse-holo slot. 11-card pack modeled as the era baseline: 1 rare-or-better + 3 uncommon + 7 common. The two basic Energy cards in each pack are Common rarity in this set, so they fold into the 7-common slot (retail listings phrase the pack as "5 common + 2 energy" = 7 commons total). Rare slot: guaranteed 1 rare per pack with holo ~1:3 packs, confirmed independently by Flipside Gaming ("Rare guaranteed 1 per pack, Holo approximately 1:3 packs") and an Elite Fourum collector thread ("holo pull rate for wotc era is 1:3 packs on average"; reported boxes ~12 holos / 36 packs = exactly 1/3, range 9-15). So rare-slot = rare-holo 0.3333 / rare 0.6667. No per-set published study exists for Base Set 2, only era-average community/anecdotal rates -> confidence LOW. Bulbapedia confirms the set's only rarity buckets are Rare Holo / Rare / Uncommon / Common (130 cards: 20 holo, 20 rare, 42 uncommon, 48 common), matching the engine buckets exactly. Each holo is equally likely (uniform within bucket), except Machamp which was deck-exclusive (not in boosters) - the engine's uniform-within-rarity assumption slightly overweights this but the EV impact is minor. Booster box = 36 packs. NO ETB (did not exist in the WOTC era) -> etbPacks null. Vintage sealed box carries a large collector premium, so EV/booster will sit well below box price - expected, rates not inflated to compensate.
